execution_order.action: Pull Batch 5 send list from your morning session records (2026-05-12)
execution_order.note: Every lead you emailed on 2026-05-12 is a LinkedIn candidate today. Name, business, town, signal, sector required for each.

execution_order.action: Execute in sector priority order: Solicitors → Estate agents → Hotels/B&Bs → Restaurants → Boutique retail → Trades
execution_order.note: Higher-sector leads have higher LinkedIn presence and higher acceptance rates. Don't work alphabetically by name — work by sector.

execution_order.action: For each lead: run primary name-based query first. If no result: run sector_discovery_fallback_query for that town+sector.
execution_order.note: Primary query = site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "[Business Name]". Fallback queries are in sector_discovery_fallback_queries below.

execution_order.action: IMAP sweep each lead before sending LinkedIn note
execution_order.note: Per lead, not bulk. If NOT_INTERESTED reply found: block LinkedIn permanently. If UNSUBSCRIBE: suppress all channels, GDPR 24h.

execution_order.action: Send connection request with note from monday_fallback_notes section in linkedin_connection_notes.json
execution_order.note: Post-reference note if posts found in last 60 days (3-4x acceptance). Generic note if no posts. 200-char hard limit — count before sending.

execution_order.action: Write linkedin_profiles.json immediately after each search — one entry per lead
execution_order.note: MANDATORY. Do not batch this. Write each entry as you complete each search. This file is how BRAIN knows LinkedIn was executed.

execution_order.action: CHANGELOG.md — log all activity same session
execution_order.note: 49 days overdue. MANDATORY before Tuesday Day-7 sends.

rules: No LinkedIn on weekdays only — today (Monday) is valid
No LinkedIn to leads 1/3/22/2/7/19/49/51/53/54 — permanently blocked
IMAP per lead before note — mandatory
200-character hard limit on every note — count in plain text editor
Do NOT reference the email sequence, SSL, mobile, audit findings, or 'Not Secure' in any note
Do NOT start a note with 'I'
If owner surname is NOT confirmed: skip LinkedIn for that lead (no guessing profiles)
Write profiles.json entry after EVERY search — not just confirmed profiles

search_queries_status: PENDING_HANDS_POPULATION — HANDS must populate this array from their 2026-05-12 Batch 5 send records. Use name-based primary queries first (format: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "[Business Name]"). If no name known: use sector_discovery_fallback_queries below for that town+sector.

sector_discovery_fallback_queries.note: Use these ONLY when name-based primary query yields no result OR when owner surname is unknown. Execute in the order shown — sector priority applies within each town.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in solicitor "Malton" "North Yorkshire" (director OR partner OR owner)
Malton.note: Highest LinkedIn likelihood in Ryedale. Malton solicitors almost always have profiles. Check posts from last 60 days — legal updates, charity, local events.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in "estate agent" "Malton" "North Yorkshire" (director OR owner OR manager)
Malton.note: Estate agents in Malton post market updates and sold listings. High post-reference opportunity.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in accountant "Malton" "North Yorkshire" (director OR partner OR owner)
Malton.note: High LinkedIn presence. Post frequently about tax deadlines, Making Tax Digital.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Malton" "North Yorkshire" retail (owner OR director OR founder)
Malton.note: Malton has a strong independent food and retail scene. Food Festival presence often generates posts.

Malton.query: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "Malton" (plumber OR electrician OR builder OR joiner OR roofer)
Malton.note: Trades — LOW LinkedIn presence. Only search if you have a full name. Skip if surname unknown.

Helmsley.query: site:linkedin.com/in hotel "Helmsley" "North Yorkshire" (owner OR director OR manager)
Helmsley.note: Highest post-reference likelihood in the entire Ryedale batch. Helmsley hospitality owners post about seasonal events, local walks, NYM Park. Check posts FIRST.

Helmsley.query: site:linkedin.com/in restaurant "Helmsley" (owner OR chef OR director)
Helmsley.note: Fine dining in the Helmsley/Harome area. Owners post menus, seasonal ingredients, supplier stories. Post-reference likely.
Helmsley estate agents cover the wider ryedale rural market — active LinkedIn presences.

Helmsley.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Helmsley" retail (owner OR director OR founder)
Helmsley.note: Independent shops — medium presence. Helmsley market day generates some activity.

Pickering.query: site:linkedin.com/in (hotel OR "B&B" OR "bed and breakfast") "Pickering" "North Yorkshire" (owner OR director)
Pickering.note: Pickering is a gateway to the North York Moors and NYMR. B&B and hotel owners post about tourism, the steam railway, seasonal offers.

Pickering.query: site:linkedin.com/in "estate agent" "Pickering" (director OR owner OR manager)
Pickering.note: Medium presence. Rural property market generates some posting activity.
Modest LinkedIn presence for Pickering restaurants — generic note if profile found.

Pickering.query: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "Pickering" (builder OR plumber OR joiner OR electrician)
Pickering.note: Trades — LOW. Only if full name confirmed. Skip if surname unknown.

Kirkbymoorside.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Kirkbymoorside" "North Yorkshire" (solicitor OR accountant OR estate agent) (owner OR director OR partner)
Kirkbymoorside.note: Try BOTH spellings — 'Kirkbymoorside' and 'Kirbymoorside' both appear in LinkedIn profiles. Small town — cast a slightly wider net.

Kirkbymoorside.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Kirkbymoorside" (hotel OR retail OR restaurant) owner director
Kirkbymoorside.note: Small market town. LinkedIn presence is modest. Use generic note if profile found.

Hovingham.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Hovingham" "North Yorkshire" (owner OR director OR manager)
Hovingham.note: Very small village. LinkedIn presence is LOW. The Worsley Arms Hotel is the main hospitality lead — search specifically if that business is in your send list. Generic note only — post-reference unlikely.

Norton-on-Derwent.query: site:linkedin.com/in "Norton" "Malton" (solicitor OR accountant OR estate agent OR builder) (owner OR director OR partner)
Norton-on-Derwent.note: Norton is effectively part of the Malton market area — use 'Norton' 'Malton' in queries rather than 'Norton-on-Derwent' alone. The full hyphenated form rarely appears in profiles.

Norton-on-Derwent.query: site:linkedin.com/in "[Full Name]" "Norton" "Malton" (builder OR plumber OR joiner OR roofer)
Norton-on-Derwent.note: Trades — LOW LinkedIn presence. Full name required. Skip if surname unknown.

primary_query_format.instruction: For each Batch 5 lead where you have a full name (first + surname), use this as your primary query before any fallback:
fallback_chain: Fallback 2: Use sector_discovery_fallback_queries above for that town+sector
Fallback 3: Skip — do NOT send note to a profile you cannot confidently identify as the owner

linkedin_profiles_json_instruction.critical: Write this file TODAY, same session as LinkedIn execution. This is the only confirmation mechanism. BRAIN cannot generate per-lead personalised notes without it.
linkedin_profiles_json_instruction.write_after_each_search: Do not batch — write one entry immediately after each search, whether or not a profile was found.

entry_schema.name: [owner name as on LinkedIn profile]
entry_schema.note_text: [exact text sent, or null if skipped]

archive_trigger.condition: Any Batch 5 lead where linkedin_profiles.json entry shows note_sent: false or entry is missing entirely
archive_trigger.action: Archive immediately as LOST_NO_RESPONSE. Log CHANGELOG same session. No deferral, no extension.
archive_trigger.rule: Non-negotiable. 49-session LinkedIn execution failure must not produce a 50th session.
